novel_features.name: Renewal pipeline
novel_features.description: List active memberships whose to-date is within a window so the renewal task is one click away.
novel_features.rationale: Joins memberships against membership-types.durationBilling locally; no ST API call returns this shape.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs the full renewal target list across the tenant, not one membership at a time.

novel_features.name: Expiring soon
novel_features.description: Every membership whose to-date falls inside a window, including already-cancelled, for lapse-recovery sweeps.
novel_features.rationale: Local SQLite filter over the synced memberships table; the ST UI shows one membership at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs both renewal and recovery targets in one list with deeply-nested data narrowed by --select.

novel_features.name: Overdue events
novel_features.description: Recurring-service events past their date on still-active memberships — what we should have already visited.
novel_features.rationale: Joins recurring-service-events against memberships and recurring-services locally; the API has no overdue filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this before scheduling work — overdue events are the SLA tab the UI never quite shows.

novel_features.name: Upcoming schedule
novel_features.description: Compact view of upcoming recurring-service events grouped by date and location.
novel_features.rationale: Joins recurring-services to events locally for businessUnitId, preferredTechnicianIds, and jobStartTime context.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when dispatch needs to pre-stage the next two weeks of recurring service work.

novel_features.name: Template drift audit
novel_features.description: Compares each active membership's recurring-services against the membership-type template, flags missing or extra services.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-joins memberships × membership-types × recurring-services in SQLite; the ST UI never shows this side-by-side.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a member's care-plan deliverables look wrong against their billed membership type.

novel_features.name: Churn risk
novel_features.description: Rule-engine score per membership: follow-up status, no payment method, lapsed bills, stale events, near to-date.
novel_features.rationale: Local SQL rule engine across three tables; no single API call returns a risk score.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent is prioritizing retention outreach across the membership book.

novel_features.name: Recurring revenue
novel_features.description: Monthly recurring revenue from memberships joined to membership-types.durationBilling, broken down by business unit and billing frequency.
novel_features.rationale: Local SQL roll-up over synced data; ST reporting can build this in the UI, not in an agent-callable shape.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you need the recurring-revenue picture without exporting a report.

novel_features.name: Memberships health
novel_features.description: One compact rollup of renewals, overdue events, drift, risk, stale services, and revenue-at-risk counts.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates five other transcendence reports into an agent-shaped priming summary.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this first in any agent session that touches memberships — it primes the model on what is broken.

novel_features.name: Stale recurring services
novel_features.description: Recurring services on active memberships with no completed event in N or more months, even when the recurrence says one should have happened.
novel_features.rationale: Joins recurring-services to event history locally; the API exposes no SLA tracker.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when auditing whether recurring service obligations are actually being delivered.

novel_features.name: Bill preview
novel_features.description: Show the next bill date and line-item amount for a single membership, resolved through its membership-type duration-billing.
novel_features.rationale: Walks memberships to membership-types.durationBilling to invoice-templates.items locally; no API call returns this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a customer asks what they're about to be charged before the invoice runs.

novel_features.name: Event quick-complete
novel_features.description: Mark a recurring-service event complete with a required job link, then refresh the local snapshot so overdue filters update.
novel_features.rationale: Wraps mark-complete with the typical reason (job link) and synchronizes the local cache in one step.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this in field-tech workflows where the event-to-job link is the whole point of marking complete.

novel_features.name: Natural-language member finder
novel_features.description: Forgiving ranked search over synced memberships using customer, importId, memo, customFields, and membership-type name.
novel_features.rationale: Domain-tuned ranking and tech-facing output shape beyond the framework search command.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent or office staffer is describing a member instead of pasting an exact ID.

narrative.headline: A focused per-module ServiceTitan CLI for Memberships — sync once, then audit renewals, overdue events, template drift, and recurring revenue the ST UI cannot.
narrative.value_prop: This CLI (servicetitan-memberships-pp-cli) mirrors every ServiceTitan Memberships v2 endpoint (customer memberships, membership types, recurring services, recurring service types, recurring service events, invoice templates, plus seven export feeds) and adds twelve novel commands that join across them in a local SQLite cache. It replaces the heavy general ST MCP for membership work with a thin search+execute MCP surface — so an agent doing renewal targeting, overdue-visit triage, churn risk scoring, or recurring-revenue rollups loads only the memberships module.
narrative.auth_narrative: ServiceTitan uses composed auth: a static ST-App-Key header (set ST_APP_KEY) plus an OAuth2 client-credentials bearer token (set ST_CLIENT_ID and ST_CLIENT_SECRET). Run servicetitan-memberships-pp-cli auth login to walk the credential setup, then set the three credentials plus ST_TENANT_ID (your numeric tenant ID). Whitespace is stripped defensively on all three OAuth env vars — a known JKA gotcha that produces opaque invalid_client 400s when a trailing newline sneaks in from a copy-paste. The integration's OAuth client needs the read scopes for every list/get plus tn.mem.memberships:w, tn.mem.invoicetemplates:w, tn.mem.recurringservices:w, and tn.mem.recurringserviceevents:w for the mutation endpoints (memberships sale + update, invoice-templates create + update, recurring-services update, recurring-service-events mark-complete + mark-incomplete).

quickstart.comment: Confirm ST_APP_KEY, bearer token, ST_TENANT_ID, and base URL before anything else.
Pull memberships, membership-types, recurring-services, recurring-service-events, recurring-service-types, and invoice-templates into the local store and snapshot membership status history.
See renewal, overdue-event, drift, risk, stale-service, and revenue-at-risk counts in one rollup.
List active memberships whose to-date is within the next 30 days for the renewal pipeline.
Show recurring-service events past their date on still-active memberships — the visits we owe.

troubleshoots.symptom: auth login returns invalid_client 400 even though ST_CLIENT_ID and ST_CLIENT_SECRET look right.
troubleshoots.fix: Re-paste the secret into a single-line env entry. ServiceTitan's OAuth server rejects trailing whitespace; this CLI strips it defensively but check that the values don't contain embedded newlines (echo -n on the value should produce no extra blank line).

troubleshoots.symptom: List commands return 403 forbidden even after auth login succeeds.
troubleshoots.fix: Your integration's OAuth client is missing a tn.mem.* scope. Check the developer-portal app and add tn.mem.memberships:r, tn.mem.membershiptypes:r, tn.mem.recurringservices:r, tn.mem.recurringserviceevents:r, tn.mem.recurringservicetypes:r, tn.mem.invoicetemplates:r. Mutation commands additionally require the :w variants.

ServiceTitan rate-limits to ~7000 requests per hour per environment per app key. The CLI's AdaptiveLimiter backs off automatically; if you hit the cap repeatedly, run sync --resources memberships,recurring-service-events to scope and let it finish before retrying the rest.

troubleshoots.symptom: drift reports template gaps but the membership UI looks correct.
troubleshoots.fix: Re-run sync — membership-types.recurring-services is sub-resource data that only refreshes on a full sync. After sync, drift's view is from the same modifiedOn cursor; if a membership type changed after your last sync, the drift report lags until you resync.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent task touches ServiceTitan's recurring-revenue book — renewal targeting, overdue-event triage, template-drift audits, churn-risk scoring, recurring-revenue rollups, or bill previews for a single customer. It replaces the general ST MCP for any membership-shaped task, with a thin code-orchestrated MCP surface that loads only the memberships module. Use it instead of the ST UI when you need joined views across memberships, membership-types, recurring-services, and events that the UI can only show one entity at a time.

recipes.title: Renewal pipeline for the month
recipes.explanation: Active memberships whose to-date is within 30 days, narrowed to the fields a renewal-outreach agent actually needs.

recipes.title: Overdue recurring-service events feeding a dispatch agent
recipes.explanation: Past-due events on active memberships with dotted-path field selection so the agent only sees what it needs to schedule.

recipes.title: Compute monthly recurring revenue by business unit
recipes.explanation: Local SQL roll-up over synced memberships joined to membership-types.durationBilling, broken down by business unit and billing frequency.

recipes.title: Find a member without an ID
recipes.explanation: Forgiving ranked search across memberships, importId, memo, custom fields, and membership-type name — useful when office staff describe a member.

recipes.title: Complete an event and refresh the snapshot
recipes.explanation: Marks event 99 complete via mark-complete with a required job link, then updates the local snapshot so overdue filters reflect the change.

trigger_phrases: preview the next bill for a member
